rulings.Case Title: JAMES TRUITT JR VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Deft Nacco Materials Handling Group, Inc'S Person Most Knowledgeable And Custodian Of Records And Request For Production Of Documents (Inadvertently Taken Off Calendar)
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, February 18, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. The hearing on plaintiffs' motion to compel the deposition of defendant NACCO's person most knowledgeable and custodian of records and to compel the production of documents is continued to February 23, 2010 to provide the parties further opportunity to resolve the issues raised by the motion. If the parties have not fully resolved the issues raised by the motion, no later than February 22, 2010 at 4:00 p.m. they need to send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org stating, without further argument, what issues remain for resolution. In the event that the parties have not informally resolved all issues raised by the motion, the parties must appear at the hearing on February 23. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LYNN R. BARANCO et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / 2nd Mo To Compel Defendant General Cable Corporation'S Person(S) Most Knowledgeable/Custodian Of Records Depositions; Request For Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, February 18, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 4. Plaintiffs LYNN BARANCO, LISA SMITH, LESLIE MARTIN and LYNN BIBB's motion to compel the deposition of defendant General Cable Corporation's person most knowledgeable and custodian of records, to compel the production of documents, and for monetary and issue sanctions is granted in part and denied in part. As to each category, General Cable is required to identify the person(s) most knowledgeable. If Mr. Gable is the sole person most knowledgeable as to any category and General Cable represents in writing that Mr. Gable has no further information that what he stated in his deposition, there will be no need for any further deposition as to that category. However, as to all other categories, a further deposition is warranted. General Cable must also make a diligent effort to locate all requested documents and provide plaintiffs with a verified response stating what documents it has been able to locate and must do so within 30 days. The requests for monetary and issue sanctions are denied because General Cable has not acted without substantial justification due to the large number of categories and document requests, the breadth of those categories and requests and the fact that the categories and requests seek information about matters that occurred almost 60 years ago. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARK JOHNSON et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS(B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Defendant Siemens Energy Automation, Inc.'S Person Most Knowledgeable And Custodian Of Records And Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, February 18, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiffs MARK JOHNSON & LYNNE JOHNSON's motion to compel the deposition of defendant Siemens Energy & Automation, Inc.'s person most knowledgeable and custodian of records and to compel the production of documents is granted. Plaintiffs are entitled to such discovery as it is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence and not otherwise subject to objection. If Siemens cannot located the documents requested after a diligent search, its custodian of records should so state. =(220/HEK)
